data.title: Genius raises Bitcoin Treasury by additional $5M
data.description:
• Genius (NYSE:GNS) said it has upped its Bitcoin (BTC-USD) purchases for its Bitcoin Treasury by an additional $5 million to 372 Bitcoin for $35 million.
  • The average price was $94,047 per Bitcoin.
  • The milestone to reach 30% of the initial target of $120 million was ahead of its target schedule, the firm said.

data.title: Ventripoint Diagnostics issues shares for payment of debenture interest
data.description:
• Ventripoint Diagnostics (TSXV:VPT:CA) on Friday said it will issue up to 83,864 common shares for the payment of $9,225.20 of interest on certain outstanding convertible debentures issued on June 28, 2025.
  • The convertible debentures bear interest at the rate of 10% per annum, payable every 6 months, said the company.
  • The shares will be issued at a deemed price of $0.11, subject to the approval of TSXV and are subject to a hold period of 4 months and one day, it added.
  • The company also said it has received TSXV approval to issue an aggregate of 32,769 common shares to settle an aggregate of $3,276 of interest in respect of convertible debentures issued on May 10, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Amylyx stock dips after pricing $60M stock offering
data.description:
Amylyx Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:AMLX) has priced a public offering of ~17.14M shares of its common stock at $3.50 a share.

In addition, Amylyx has granted the underwriter a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional ~2.57M shares of its common stock at the public offering price per share, less underwriting discounts and commissions

The gross proceeds to Amylyx from this offering are expected to be ~$60M, before deducting underwriting discounts and commissions and offering expenses and excluding any exercise of the underwriter’s option to purchase additional shares.

The pharma firm will use the net proceeds from this offering, together with existing cash, cash equivalents and marketable securities, to advance preparations for commercialization and pipeline programs, for working capital and for general corporate purposes.

The offering is expected to close on or about January 13, 2025, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

Shares of Amylyx Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:AMLX) fell 3.18% premarket on Friday.

Source: Press Release

data.title: CNB Financial to acquire ESSA Bancorp in an all-stock deal
data.description:
Bank holding company CNB Financial (NASDAQ:CCNE) is set to acquire ESSA Bancorp (NASDAQ:ESSA) in an all-stock deal, valuing the latter at ~$214M.

CNB shares were trading +1.00% Friday pre-market at $24.35, while ESSA was 9.93% up to $20.48.

ESSA shareholders will receive 0.8547 CNB shares for each share that they hold.

The transaction consideration comes to ~$21.10 per ESSA share.

The two companies announced on Friday a definitive agreement for the merger.

The combined company is expected to have ~$8B in assets, $7B in deposits, and $6B in loans.

The transaction is expected to be a tax-free exchange for shareholders of ESSA.

The merger is expected to be ~35% accretive to CNB's diluted earnings per share in 2026.

Tangible book value per share dilution at transaction close is projected to be 15%.

The transaction is expected to close in Q3.

Source: Press Release

data.title: AppFolio cut to Sector-Weight at KeyBanc
data.description:
• AppFolio (NASDAQ:APPF) was downgraded to Sector-Weight from Overweight at KeyBanc Capital Markets on Friday, as the investment firm expects growth in 2025 to be lower than previously thought.
  • Shares fell 1.3% in premarket trading.
  • "Since our upgrade on 4/7/22, AppFolio has outperformed the Nasdaq (+124.9% vs. +42.1%, respectively), attributed to its strong execution in driving share gains, expanding ARPU, and meaningfully improving margins," analyst Jason Celino wrote in a note to clients. "That said, we are lowering our 2025E growth expectations due to more muted [near-term] units growth outlook and view 2025 as a transitional year as the Company faces tough comps and leadership changes. We continue to view APPF as a high quality business, but with limited [near-term] catalysts and shares fairly valued on a FCF basis, we view current risk-reward as more balanced, hence, we are downgrading to [Sector-Weight]."

data.title: Stock index futures fall sharply as jobs report comes in hot
data.description:
Stock index futures tumbled on Friday, as investors digested the hotter than expected jobs report.

S&P 500 futures (SPX) -1.1%, Nasdaq 100 futures (US100:IND) -1.2% and Dow futures (INDU) -1%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 9 basis points to 4.78%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 11 basis point to 4.38%.

U.S. markets were shut on Thursday for a national day of mourning for former U.S. President Jimmy Carter.

The next test for markets will now be the U.S. jobs report for December, Deutsche Bank's Henry Allen said.

Payrolls came in more than expected in December, pushing down the unemployment rate.

"The payrolls number is derived from a survey that has a 43% response rate, and that poor representation means significant revisions are much more likely. The market is looking for a fairly dull report overall—some moderation in job creation with a stable unemployment rate," UBS' Paul Donovan said.

The January consumer sentiment is also expected to land during market hours, and is forecasted to come in at 74.

U.S. Michigan consumer sentiment is still vulnerable to partisan bias. The recent improvement is mainly due to surging Republican sentiment. Republicans are more emotional than Democrats, pushing the overall index higher. Partisan bias does affect the inflation expectations component, Donovan added.

data.title: Lantheus increases to 12.16% shareholding in Radiopharm Theranostics
data.description:
• Lantheus increases to 12.16% shareholding in Radiopharm Theranostics (NASDAQ:RADX) with a $5M placement (A$8.0 million).
  • The placement of 133 million shares was completed at an issue price of A$0.060 per share, representing a 150% premium to the last traded price of RAD shares (A$0.024). Settlement is expected to occur within 7 days.
  • Filing

data.title: Canada's unemployment eases to 6.70% in December
data.description:
The unemployment rate in Canada decreased to 6.70% in December from 6.80% in November 2024.

ETFs: (EWC), (FLCA).

Currency: (USD:CAD)

data.title: Savers Value Village guides Q4 sales above estimates
data.description:
Savers Value Village (NYSE:SVV) expects Q4 net sales to rise 5% to $402M (consensus estimate: $397.04M), with comparable store sales up 1.6%.

Based on preliminary estimates, constant currency net sales for the fourth quarter increased 6% to $405.9M, with U.S. sales +10.5% and Canada sales -0.2%.

Net sales for fiscal 2024 are predicted to grow 2.5% to $1.54B ($1.53B estimate). Comparable store sales for fiscal 2024 are expected to fall 0.1%, with the U.S. increasing 2.7% and Canada decreasing 4%.

The thrift store operator also reaffirmed its previously provided fiscal 2024 adjusted EBITDA outlook of approximately $290M-$300M.

CEO Mark Walsh commented, “Our sales performance strengthened in the fourth quarter in both the U.S. and Canada. We are pleased with our double-digit total revenue growth in the U.S., driven by accelerating new store growth and strong comparable store sales. And we made progress in our Canada business despite continuing Canadian macroeconomic headwinds. We remain on track with our new store growth plans and look forward to opening 25-30 new stores in 2025.”

Shares of Savers Value Village (NYSE:SVV) were up 1% in premarket trading.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Glass Lewis recommends all four Mantle Ridge board candidates for Air Products
data.description:
Air Products (NYSE:APD) +2.9% pre-market Friday after proxy advisory firm Glass Lewis said shareholders should elect all four board candidates proposed by activist investor Mantle Ridge.

"Following years of poor performance fueled by a spate of high-cost, low-visibility strategic expeditions pointedly departing from APD's core risk profile - and in view of a functionally vacant succession framework slated to leave CEO Seifi Ghasemi in an indefinite position of influence - we consider Mantle Ridge has provided shareholders with a clear, credible and proportionate alternative backed by suitably experienced, independent candidates," Glass Lewis wrote.

"Investors would be best served supporting significant alterations" to the Air Products (APD) board, according to Glass Lewis, seeing a strong case to suggest APD investors have ceded significant value as a direct extension of management's capital-intensive non-core strategy, and should, in our view, have very little confidence in the idea that long-serving members of the board actively committed to the further pursuit of that strategy are likely to serve as effective change agents here."

Mantle Ridge has said Air Products (APD) has "dramatically underperformed over the past five years relative to its peers and its potential, a direct result of the poor stewardship" of its management and board, and is asking shareholders to elect its four nominees, including the hedge fund's founder Paul Hilal, to the nine-member board to help improve operations and ultimately find a new CEO.
